biblical.framework: North-to-South migration from the Nile Valley
biblical.evidence: Sudden appearance of Urewe pottery, metallurgical skills carried from advanced civilizations, oral traditions citing 'Misri'.

conventional.framework: Gradual West-to-East expansion over thousands of years
conventional.evidence: Linguistic spread from West Africa, evolutionary assumptions of independent development.

comparisons.resolution: The biblical model accounts for the rapidity of skill transfer across vast distances, supported by African oral traditions that secular models dismiss.
